segments.text: Nebuchadnezzar the king made an image of gold, whose height was sixty cubits, [^5] and its width six cubits. He set it up in the plain of Dura, in the province of Babylon.

Then Nebuchadnezzar the king sent to gather together the local governors, the deputies, and the governors, the judges, the treasurers, the counselors, the sheriffs, and all the rulers of the provinces, to come to the dedication of the image which Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up.

Then the local governors, the deputies, and the governors, the judges, the treasurers, the counselors, the sheriffs, and all the rulers of the provinces, were gathered together to the dedication of the image that Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up; and they stood before the image that Nebuchadnezzar had set up.
Then the herald cried aloud,
To you it is commanded, peoples, nations, and languages,
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: that whenever you hear the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe, and all kinds of music, you fall down and worship the golden image that Nebuchadnezzar the king has set up.
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: Whoever doesn’t fall down and worship shall be cast into the middle of a burning fiery furnace the same hour.
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: Therefore at that time, when all the peoples heard the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe, and all kinds of music, all the peoples, the nations, and the languages, fell down and worshiped the golden image that Nebuchadnezzar the king had set up.
Therefore at that time certain Chaldeans came near, and brought accusation against the Jews.
They answered Nebuchadnezzar the king,
O king, live for ever!
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: You, O king, have made a decree that every man who hears the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe, and all kinds of music shall fall down and worship the golden image;
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: and whoever doesn’t fall down and worship shall be cast into the middle of a burning fiery furnace.
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: There are certain Jews whom you have appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. These men, O king, have not respected you. They don’t serve your gods, and don’t worship the golden image which you have set up.
segments.speaker: The herald

segments.text: Then Nebuchadnezzar in rage and fury commanded that Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego be brought. Then these men were brought before the king.
Nebuchadnezzar answered them,
Is it on purpose, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, that you don’t serve my god, nor worship the golden image which I have set up?
segments.speaker: Nebuchadnezzar

segments.text: Now if you are ready whenever you hear the sound of the horn, flute, zither, lyre, harp, pipe, and all kinds of music to fall down and worship the image which I have made, good; but if you don’t worship, you shall be cast the same hour into the middle of a burning fiery furnace. Who is that god who will deliver you out of my hands?
segments.speaker: Nebuchadnezzar

segments.text: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego answered the king,
Nebuchadnezzar, we have no need to answer you in this matter.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: If it happens, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace; and he will deliver us out of your hand, O king.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: But if not, let it be known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods or worship the golden image which you have set up.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Then Nebuchadnezzar was full of fury, and the form of his appearance was changed against Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. He spoke, and commanded that they should heat the furnace seven times more than it was usually heated.
He commanded certain mighty men who were in his army to bind Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, and to cast them into the burning fiery furnace.
Then these men were bound in their pants, their tunics, their mantles, and their other clothes, and were cast into the middle of the burning fiery furnace.
Therefore because the king’s commandment was urgent, and the furnace exceedingly hot, the flame of the fire killed those men who took up Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego.
These three men, Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, fell down bound into the middle of the burning fiery furnace.

text: THE SONG OF THE THREE HOLY CHILDREN3:24 The Song of the Three Holy Children is an addition to Daniel found in the Greek Septuagint but not found in the traditional Hebrew text of Daniel. This portion is recognized as Deuterocanonical Scripture by the Roman Catholic, Greek Orthodox, and Russian Orthodox Churches. It is found inserted between Daniel 3:23 and Daniel 3:24 of the traditional Hebrew Bible. Here, the verses after 23 from the Hebrew Bible are numbered starting at 91 to make room for these verses.

segments.text: They walked in the midst of the fire, praising God, and blessing the Lord.
Then Azarias stood, and prayed like this. Opening his mouth in the midst of the fire he said,
Blessed are you, O Lord, you God of our fathers! Your name is worthy to be praised and glorified for evermore;
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: for you are righteous in all the things that you have done. Yes, all your works are true. Your ways are right, and all your judgments are truth.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: In all the things that you have brought upon us, and upon the holy city of our fathers, Jerusalem, you have executed true judgments. For according to truth and justice you have brought all these things upon us because of our sins.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: For we have sinned and committed iniquity in departing from you.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: In all things we have trespassed, and not obeyed your commandments or kept them. We haven’t done as you have commanded us, that it might go well with us.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Therefore all that you have brought upon us, and everything that you have done to us, you have done in true judgment.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: You delivered us into the hands of lawless enemies, most hateful rebels, and to an unjust king who is the most wicked in all the world.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: And now we can’t open our mouth. Shame and reproach have come on your servants and those who worship you.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Don’t utterly deliver us up, for your name’s sake. Don’t annul your covenant.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Don’t cause your mercy to depart from us, for the sake of Abraham who is loved by you, and for the sake of Isaac your servant, and Israel your holy one,
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: to whom you promised that you would multiply their offspring as the stars of the sky, and as the sand that is on the sea shore.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: For we, O Lord, have become less than any nation, and are brought low this day in all the world because of our sins.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: There isn’t at this time prince, or prophet, or leader, or burnt offering, or sacrifice, or oblation, or incense, or place to offer before you, and to find mercy.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Nevertheless in a contrite heart and a humble spirit let us be accepted,
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: like the burnt offerings of rams and bullocks, and like ten thousands of fat lambs. So let our sacrifice be in your sight this day, that we may wholly go after you, for they shall not be ashamed who put their trust in you.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: And now we follow you with all our heart. We fear you, and seek your face.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Put us not to shame; but deal with us after your kindness, and according to the multitude of your mercy.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Deliver us also according to your marvelous works, and give glory to your name, O Lord. Let all those who harm your servants be confounded.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Let them be ashamed of all their power and might, and let their strength be broken.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Let them know that you are the Lord, the only God, and glorious over the whole world.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: The king’s servants who put them in didn’t stop making the furnace hot with naphtha, pitch, tinder, and small wood,
so that the flame streamed out forty nine cubits above the furnace.
It spread and burned those Chaldeans whom it found around the furnace.
But the angel of the Lord came down into the furnace together with Azarias and his fellows, and he struck the flame of the fire out of the furnace,
and made the midst of the furnace as it had been a moist whistling wind, so that the fire didn’t touch them at all. It neither hurt nor troubled them.
Then the three, as out of one mouth, praised, glorified, and blessed God in the furnace, saying,
Blessed are you, O Lord, you God of our fathers, to be praised and exalted above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Blessed is your glorious and holy name, to be praised and exalted above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Blessed are you in the temple of your holy glory, to be praised and glorified above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Blessed are you who see the depths and sit upon the cherubim, to be praised and exalted above all forever.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Blessed are you on the throne of your kingdom, to be praised and extolled above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: Blessed are you in the firmament of heaven, to be praised and glorified forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you works of the Lord, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you heavens, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all for ever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you angels of the Lord, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you waters that are above the sky, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you powers of the Lord, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you sun and moon, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you stars of heaven, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O every shower and dew, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you winds, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you fire and heat, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you dews and storms of snow, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you nights and days, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you light and darkness, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you cold and heat, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you frost and snow, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you lightnings and clouds, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O let the earth bless the Lord! Let it praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you mountains and hills, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you things that grow on the earth, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: [^6] O sea and rivers, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you springs, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you whales and all that move in the waters, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you birds of the air, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you beasts and cattle, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you children of men, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O let Israel bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you priests of the Lord, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you servants of the Lord, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you spirits and souls of the righteous, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O you who are holy and humble of heart, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah, bless the Lord! Praise and exalt him above all forever; for he has rescued us from Hades, and saved us from the hand of death! He has delivered us out of the midst of the furnace and burning flame. He has delivered us out of the midst of the fire.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; for his mercy is forever.
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: O all you who worship the Lord, bless the God of gods, praise him, and give him thanks; for his mercy is forever!
segments.speaker: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego

segments.text: [^7] Then Nebuchadnezzar the king was astonished and rose up in haste. He spoke and said to his counselors,
Didn’t we cast three men bound into the middle of the fire?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They answered the king,
True, O king.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He answered,
Look, I see four men loose, walking in the middle of the fire, and they are unharmed. The appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then Nebuchadnezzar came near to the mouth of the burning fiery furnace. He spoke and said,
Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, you servants of the Most High God, come out, and come here!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego came out of the middle of the fire.

The local governors, the deputies, and the governors, and the king’s counselors, being gathered together, saw these men, that the fire had no power on their bodies. The hair of their head wasn’t singed. Their pants weren’t changed. The smell of fire wasn’t even on them.
Nebuchadnezzar spoke and said,

Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who has sent his angel and delivered his servants who trusted in him, and have changed the king’s word, and have yielded their bodies, that they might not serve nor worship any god, except their own God.
segments.speaker: Nebuchadnezzar

segments.text: Therefore I make a decree, that every people, nation, and language, who speak anything evil against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, shall be cut in pieces, and their houses shall be made a dunghill, because there is no other god who is able to deliver like this.
segments.speaker: Nebuchadnezzar

segments.text: Then the king promoted Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the province of Babylon.
